Class Board

java.lang.Object
com.cluegame.model.Board
public class Board extends Object
Represents the Clue! game board. The board is a 24x25 grid of squares loaded from an external config file.
Author:
Thanh Shaw

  • Constructor Details

    • Board

      public Board()
      Constructs a new Board and initialises all rooms.

  • Method Details

    • initialiseRooms

      private void initialiseRooms()
      Creates all 9 rooms on the board.

    • initialiseGrid

      private void initialiseGrid()
      Initialises the grid with the classic Cluedo board layout. Places all 9 rooms, blocked wall squares, doors and the 6 starting positions. A config file loaded via loadFromFile() can override this.

    • fillRoom

      private void fillRoom(String roomName, int r1, int c1, int r2, int c2)
      Fills a rectangular region with ROOM squares and links them to the named Room object.
      Parameters:
      roomName - the room to assign these squares to
      r1 - top row of the rectangle (inclusive)
      c1 - left column (inclusive)
      r2 - bottom row (inclusive)
      c2 - right column (inclusive)

    • fillBlocked

      private void fillBlocked(int r1, int c1, int r2, int c2)
      Fills a rectangular region with BLOCKED squares.
      Parameters:
      r1 - top row (inclusive)
      c1 - left column (inclusive)
      r2 - bottom row (inclusive)
      c2 - right column (inclusive)

    • addDoor

      private void addDoor(String roomName, int row, int col)
      Marks a corridor square as a door for the given room and registers it with the Room object. The door square itself stays as CORRIDOR so players can stand on it.
      Parameters:
      roomName - the room this door belongs to
      row - door row
      col - door column

    • setStart

      private void setStart(int row, int col)
      Marks a square as a player starting position.
      Parameters:
      row - start row
      col - start column

    • printBoard

      public void printBoard()
      Prints a text representation of the board to standard output. Each room is shown with a unique letter (see legend below the grid). Corridors = '.', blocked = '#', start = 'S', doors = '*'.

    • printBoard

      public void printBoard(List<Player> players)
      Prints the board with player positions overlaid. Players in corridors are shown as numbered tokens (1-6). Players in rooms are listed in a summary below the grid.
      Parameters:
      players - the list of players to display on the board

    • loadFromFile

      public void loadFromFile(String filePath)
      Loads the board layout from an external text config file. Each character in the file maps to a square type: C = Corridor, R = Room, B = Blocked, S = Start
      Parameters:
      filePath - path to the board config file

    • getSquare

      public Square getSquare(int row, int col)
      Returns the square at the given position.
      Parameters:
      row - the row index
      col - the column index
      Returns:
      the Square at that position

    • getRoom

      public Room getRoom(String name)
      Returns a room by name.
      Parameters:
      name - the room name
      Returns:
      the Room object

    • getRooms

      public Map<String,Room> getRooms()
      Returns all rooms on the board.
      Returns:
      map of room names to Room objects

    • isValidMove

      public boolean isValidMove(int fromRow, int fromCol, int toRow, int toCol)
      Checks whether moving from one square to another is valid. A move is valid if the destination is within bounds, exactly one step horizontally or vertically from the source, and not a BLOCKED square. Does not check player collisions — use the overloaded version for that.
      Parameters:
      fromRow - current row
      fromCol - current column
      toRow - destination row
      toCol - destination column
      Returns:
      true if the move is valid

    • isValidMove

      public boolean isValidMove(int fromRow, int fromCol, int toRow, int toCol, List<Player> allPlayers)
      Checks whether moving from one square to another is valid, including player collision. Same rules as the basic version but also blocks moves into ROOM squares (must enter via doors) and into corridor squares occupied by another player.
      Parameters:
      fromRow - current row
      fromCol - current column
      toRow - destination row
      toCol - destination column
      allPlayers - all players for corridor occupancy checks
      Returns:
      true if the move is valid

    • isDoor

      public boolean isDoor(int row, int col)
      Returns true if the square at the given position is a door. A door is a CORRIDOR square that has a room reference set on it.
      Parameters:
      row - the row to check
      col - the column to check
      Returns:
      true if the square is a door

    • getRoomAt

      public Room getRoomAt(int row, int col)
      Returns the Room associated with the square at the given position, or null if the square has no room (e.g. plain corridor, blocked). Works for both door squares and room-interior squares.
      Parameters:
      row - the row
      col - the column
      Returns:
      the Room at that position, or null
